title: Space Tech Briefing — June 24, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! It's June 24, 2026, and you are locked into your ultimate daily dose of cosmic currents and technological triumphs! Let's dive right into the latest from the final frontier.

Kicking things off with brand new developments today! France's CNES space agency is making waves, literally, with its AVALON scientific campaign. Right now, as we speak, 25 small sounding balloons are being launched from the CNES center in Aire-sur-l'Adour. This isn't just a picturesque sight; it's a critical mission! These balloons are designed to calibrate satellites, meticulously measure greenhouse gases, and lay the groundwork for future space missions. It's all about precision data for our planet and preparing for what's next in orbit!

And as we anticipate more launches and discoveries, let's take a moment to look back at a major recent development that continues to propel Europe's space ambitions forward! Just last week, on Wednesday, June 17, Europe's mighty Ariane 6 rocket notched another incredible success! Its eighth launch was a triumph, deploying 36 satellites for the Amazon Leo constellation into orbit. This mission was particularly significant because it showcased a new variant: the Ariane 64, flying with four powerful P160C boosters. Remember, Ariane 6 is built for versatility, designed from the ground up for continuous upgrades. We saw its inaugural flight back in July 2024 with two P120C engines, and its first four-booster flight, the A64 version, in February 2026. This latest launch with the P160Cs further cements its role as a flexible, powerful workhorse for Europe!

That's all for your morning space briefing, folks! Keep looking up, keep dreaming big, and we'll connect again tomorrow with more updates from the final frontier. Have a fantastic day!
